articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds 'Right to Digital Identity' as Fundamental Right
articles.explainer:
• In a landmark judgment, the Supreme Court of India declared the 'Right to Digital Identity' as an intrinsic part of the fundamental Right to Privacy under Article 21.
• The ruling mandates the government to ensure robust data protection frameworks for all digital platforms and services.
• It emphasizes that individuals have control over their digital footprint, including the right to access, correct, and erase their personal data.
• The judgment sets a precedent for future legislation on data governance, digital public infrastructure, and the ethical use of AI.
• It has significant implications for both government agencies handling citizen data and private entities operating in the digital economy.

prelimsPoints: Article 21: Protection of Life and Personal Liberty.

mainsPoints: Critically analyze the implications of the 'Right to Digital Identity' ruling on government surveillance and citizen data collection.
Discuss how this judgment will necessitate amendments or stricter enforcement of the DPDP Act, 2023.
Examine the challenges and opportunities for India's digital economy in light of enhanced data protection requirements.
Evaluate the balance between national security interests and individual digital privacy in a rapidly evolving technological landscape.
Suggest measures to ensure effective implementation of the ruling across various sectors.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration of a digital identity icon with legal scales in the background, symbolizing the Supreme Court's judgment.
articles.trivia: India's Aadhaar system, the world's largest biometric ID program, has been at the forefront of digital identity debates globally.

articles.headline: Tripura Assembly Speaker Biswa Bandhu Sen Passes Away at 72
articles.explainer:
• Biswa Bandhu Sen, the sitting Speaker of the Tripura Legislative Assembly, passed away at the age of 72 due to a brain hemorrhage.
• Sen had a long and distinguished career in state politics, serving as an MLA since 2008.
• He held the position of Deputy Speaker from 2018 to 2023 before being elected Speaker in 2023.
• His demise marks a significant loss for the political landscape of Tripura.
• Condolences poured in from across the political spectrum, highlighting his contributions to public service.

articles.imageDescription: Official portrait of Biswa Bandhu Sen, Speaker of the Tripura Legislative Assembly.
articles.trivia: The Speaker of a Legislative Assembly holds a position of immense authority and impartiality, presiding over the house and ensuring decorum.

articles.headline: RBI MPC Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate Amidst Easing Inflation Concerns
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) unanimously decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at 6.5% in its December review.
• The decision comes amidst a gradual easing of retail inflation, which has moved closer to the RBI's target band, and moderate economic growth.
• RBI Governor highlighted global uncertainties and the need to remain vigilant against potential inflationary pressures from food prices and crude oil.
• The MPC reiterated its commitment to aligning inflation with the 4% target while supporting growth.
• Analysts anticipate a possible rate cut in early 2026 if inflation continues its downward trajectory and global economic conditions stabilize.

articles.imageDescription: Reserve Bank of India building facade, symbolizing monetary policy decisions.
articles.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee was established in 2016 to bring more transparency and accountability to monetary policy decisions.

articles.headline: India-ASEAN Dialogue Emphasizes Enhanced Maritime Security Cooperation
articles.explainer:
• India and the Association of Southeast Asian Nations (ASEAN) concluded their annual strategic dialogue with a strong focus on bolstering maritime security.
• Discussions centered on joint patrols, information sharing, and capacity building to counter piracy, illegal fishing, and enhance disaster response in the Indo-Pacific.
• Both sides reiterated their commitment to a free, open, and rules-based Indo-Pacific region, adhering to international law, particularly UNCLOS.
• The dialogue also explored avenues for greater economic integration and connectivity projects, including the India-Myanmar-Thailand Trilateral Highway extension.
• This enhanced cooperation is seen as crucial for regional stability and economic prosperity amidst evolving geopolitical dynamics.

articles.headline: Government Unveils National Circular Economy Policy to Boost Sustainability
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change launched the comprehensive National Circular Economy Policy (NCEP) 2025.
• The policy aims to minimize waste, maximize resource utilization, and promote sustainable production and consumption patterns across various sectors.
• Key pillars include extended producer responsibility (EPR), material efficiency, waste-to-wealth initiatives, and green procurement.
• It sets specific targets for resource recovery rates in sectors like plastics, electronics, construction & demolition, and automotive.
• The NCEP is expected to drive green innovation, create new jobs, and reduce India's reliance on virgin resources, contributing to climate goals.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches Advanced Communication Satellite 'GSAT-N3'
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully launched its next-generation communication satellite, GSAT-N3, from Sriharikota.
• Carried aboard the GSLV Mk-III (LVM3) rocket, GSAT-N3 is designed to provide high-throughput communication services for over 15 years.
• The satellite will enhance broadband connectivity, disaster management support, and satellite-based navigation services across India and neighboring regions.
• This mission marks a significant step in India's efforts to augment its indigenous satellite communication capabilities.
• The advanced features include higher capacity transponders and improved spectral efficiency, crucial for meeting growing data demands.

articles.trivia: GSLV Mk-III, now designated LVM3 (Launch Vehicle Mark-3), is ISRO's heaviest launch vehicle, capable of launching 4-tonne class satellites into GTO.

articles.headline: Parliament Passes 'Prison Reforms Bill, 2025' to Modernize Correctional Facilities
articles.explainer:
• Both Houses of Parliament passed the landmark 'Prison Reforms Bill, 2025', replacing the colonial-era Prisons Act of 1894.
• The new legislation focuses on the reformation and rehabilitation of prisoners, emphasizing vocational training, education, and psychological support.
• It mandates improved living conditions, healthcare access, and legal aid for inmates, aligning with international human rights standards.
• The Bill also introduces provisions for the use of technology in prison management, such as e-prisons and video conferencing for trials.
• States will now have a framework to adopt modern prison administration practices, addressing issues of overcrowding and staff training.

articles.headline: India's Industrial Production Records Moderate Growth in Q3 FY26
articles.explainer:
• India's Index of Industrial Production (IIP) registered a moderate growth of 4.2% in the third quarter (October-December) of FY26.
• The manufacturing sector remained the primary driver, albeit with mixed performance across sub-sectors.
• Mining output showed robust expansion, while electricity generation maintained a steady pace.
• The data indicates a cautious recovery trend, influenced by global demand fluctuations and domestic consumption patterns.
• Government initiatives like Make in India and PLI schemes are expected to further boost industrial activity in the coming quarters.

articles.headline: India Reaffirms Commitment to UN Peacekeeping with Enhanced Contributions
articles.explainer:
• India, a leading contributor to UN Peacekeeping missions, reaffirmed its commitment to global peace and security with additional pledges of personnel and equipment.
• During a high-level UN meeting, India emphasized the need for comprehensive reforms in UN Peacekeeping operations, including greater representation for troop-contributing countries.
• Discussions focused on addressing the evolving nature of conflicts, enhancing peacekeepers' safety, and integrating technology into missions.
• India highlighted its long-standing tradition of supporting UN efforts, having contributed over 260,000 troops to 49 missions since 1948.
• The pledges include specialized units for cybersecurity and medical support, reflecting modern peacekeeping demands.

articles.trivia: India is one of the few countries to have deployed all-women contingents in UN Peacekeeping missions, setting a precedent for gender equality.

articles.headline: India Announces Ambitious New Targets for Renewable Energy Capacity by 2030
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy announced enhanced targets for India's renewable energy (RE) capacity, aiming for 550 GW by 2030.
• This revised target includes significant increases in solar, wind, and hydropower capacities, demonstrating India's commitment to climate action.
• The announcement emphasizes accelerating green hydrogen production and energy storage solutions to support grid stability.
• Policy measures will focus on incentivizing private investment, streamlining regulatory approvals, and promoting domestic manufacturing of RE components.
• The move aligns with India's nationally determined contributions (NDCs) under the Paris Agreement and aims to reduce reliance on fossil fuels.

articles.headline: National Health Mission 2025: Progress Report Highlights Achievements and Gaps
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare released its annual progress report for the National Health Mission (NHM) 2025, detailing achievements and identifying areas for improvement.
• The report highlighted significant strides in maternal and child health indicators, including reduced Infant Mortality Rate (IMR) and Maternal Mortality Ratio (MMR).
• Focus areas for improvement include strengthening primary healthcare infrastructure, increasing human resources in rural areas, and addressing non-communicable diseases (NCDs).
• Digital health initiatives, such as the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM), showed accelerated adoption, enhancing healthcare access and efficiency.
• The report also underscored the need for greater public-private partnerships to achieve universal health coverage goals.

articles.headline: Accelerated Border Management Infrastructure Development in Northeast
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Home Affairs announced accelerated plans for border management infrastructure development along India's northeastern frontiers.
• The initiative includes constructing new border outposts, upgrading existing surveillance technology, and improving road connectivity to remote areas.
• The focus is on enhancing security, facilitating legitimate trade, and addressing cross-border challenges like illegal immigration and smuggling.
• This development is part of a broader strategy to strengthen India's presence and foster economic growth in the border regions.
• Local communities are being engaged to ensure inclusive development and address their specific needs while improving security.

articles.headline: SEBI Introduces Stricter Disclosure Norms for ESG Funds
articles.explainer:
• The Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) announced tighter disclosure and compliance norms for Environment, Social, and Governance (ESG) funds.
• The new regulations aim to prevent 'greenwashing' and ensure greater transparency and accountability in ESG investing.
• Asset Management Companies (AMCs) managing ESG funds will be required to disclose detailed methodologies, criteria for stock selection, and impact assessment reports.
• This move is expected to boost investor confidence in ESG products and channel more capital towards genuinely sustainable investments.
• The framework also includes provisions for independent third-party verification of ESG claims and standardized reporting formats.

articles.headline: National Quantum Mission Achieves Breakthrough in Quantum Computing Hardware
articles.explainer:
• Scientists under India's National Quantum Mission (NQM) announced a significant breakthrough in developing stable quantum computing hardware.
• Researchers at a premier institute successfully demonstrated a 32-qubit superconducting quantum processor with enhanced coherence times.
• This achievement positions India among the few nations capable of developing advanced quantum computing technologies.
• The breakthrough is crucial for advancing quantum computing applications in drug discovery, materials science, and cryptography.
• NQM aims to make India a leader in quantum technologies, fostering indigenous research and development across various quantum domains.

articles.headline: Lokpal Strengthens Anti-Corruption Framework with New Guidelines for Public Officials
articles.explainer:

• The guidelines mandate stricter compliance with asset declaration rules, enhanced vigilance mechanisms, and quicker disposal of corruption complaints.
• They also emphasize a proactive approach to prevent corruption through systemic reforms and technology integration.
• The move is expected to improve transparency and accountability in public administration, fostering a culture of integrity.
• The Lokpal reiterated its commitment to acting against corruption at the highest levels of government, ensuring ethical governance.

articles.headline: Coastal Regulation Zone Amendments to Boost 'Blue Economy' and Coastal Development
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change announced significant amendments to the Coastal Regulation Zone (CRZ) Notification.
• The amendments aim to balance environmental protection with the promotion of 'Blue Economy' initiatives and sustainable coastal development.
• Key changes include simplified procedures for coastal infrastructure projects, promotion of eco-tourism, and clearer guidelines for local communities' livelihoods.
• The revised CRZ framework seeks to facilitate investment in port development, renewable energy projects (offshore wind), and aquaculture.
• Environmental safeguards, including stringent EIA norms and protection of ecologically sensitive areas, remain central to the amended policy.

articles.headline: New Initiatives Launched to Preserve Endangered Tribal Languages in India
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Tribal Affairs, in collaboration with linguistic experts, launched a series of initiatives to document and preserve India's endangered tribal languages.
• The program includes digital archiving of oral traditions, development of teaching materials, and training of community linguists.
• It aims to revitalize linguistic diversity, which is intrinsically linked to the cultural heritage and identity of tribal communities.
• The initiative will focus on languages with fewer than 10,000 speakers, leveraging technology to create accessible resources.
• This effort aligns with India's commitment to protecting indigenous cultures and promoting multilingualism.

articles.headline: India's Digital Payments Ecosystem Records Record Transactions in Q3 FY26
articles.explainer:
• India's digital payments ecosystem witnessed an unprecedented surge, recording over 45 billion transactions in the third quarter of FY26.
• UPI (Unified Payments Interface) continued to be the dominant force, accounting for a major share of the transactions, driven by merchant payments and peer-to-peer transfers.
• The growth indicates increasing adoption of digital financial services across both urban and semi-urban areas, fueled by government initiatives and fintech innovation.
• This robust performance highlights India's leadership in digital public infrastructure and financial inclusion.
• The expansion of RuPay credit cards and wallet-based payments also contributed significantly to the overall growth.

articles.headline: India-EU Free Trade Agreement Negotiations Show Positive Momentum
articles.explainer:
• Negotiations for the India-European Union Free Trade Agreement (FTA) have shown significant positive momentum in the latest round of talks.
• Both sides reported progress on key areas including market access for goods, services, and investment protection.
• The FTA aims to boost bilateral trade and investment, creating new opportunities for businesses and fostering economic growth.
• Remaining contentious issues primarily revolve around tariffs on sensitive agricultural products and intellectual property rights.
• A joint statement expressed optimism for a comprehensive and mutually beneficial agreement by late 2026, marking a major milestone in India-EU relations.

articles.headline: India's Deep Ocean Mission: Samudrayaan Project Nears Crucial Submersible Trials
articles.explainer:
• India's ambitious Deep Ocean Mission, particularly the Samudrayaan project, is nearing crucial manned submersible trials in early 2026.
• The 'Matsya 6000' submersible, designed to carry three personnel to a depth of 6,000 meters, has completed integration and shallow-water tests.
• This mission aims to explore deep-sea resources like polymetallic nodules and conduct biodiversity studies in the central Indian Ocean basin.
• It marks a significant step towards India's capability in deep-sea exploration and sustainable utilization of ocean resources.
• The project also involves the development of advanced ocean technologies and a comprehensive understanding of marine ecosystems.

articles.headline: Namami Gange Mission Enters Phase III with Focus on Sustainable River Basin Management
articles.explainer:
• The Namami Gange Mission has officially entered its third phase, shifting focus from initial cleanup efforts to long-term sustainable river basin management.
• Phase III emphasizes the integration of ecological restoration, biodiversity conservation, and community participation in maintaining the Ganga's health.
• New projects include advanced wastewater treatment plants, wetland rejuvenation, afforestation along the river banks, and promotion of eco-friendly agricultural practices.
• The mission aims to establish a robust governance framework for inter-state and inter-sectoral coordination for holistic river management.
• This phase also prioritizes scientific research and data-driven approaches to monitor river health and address emerging challenges like climate change impacts.

articles.headline: India and Iran Review Progress on Chabahar Port Expansion, Regional Connectivity
articles.explainer:
• India and Iran held a high-level review meeting to assess the progress of the Chabahar Port development project and its role in regional connectivity.
• Both nations reiterated their commitment to expediting the operationalization of the Shahid Beheshti terminal, managed by India.
• Discussions focused on streamlining logistical challenges, attracting further investment, and expanding the port's role as a transit hub for Afghanistan and Central Asian countries.
• The port is crucial for India to bypass Pakistan for trade with Afghanistan and Central Asia, enhancing its strategic influence.
• The meeting also explored avenues for broader bilateral economic cooperation, including energy and infrastructure projects.

articles.headline: NULM Report Highlights Positive Impact on Urban Poor Livelihoods
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs released a comprehensive report on the impact of the National Urban Livelihoods Mission (NULM).
• The report highlighted significant achievements in skill development, self-employment, and access to financial services for the urban poor.
• Over 10 million urban poor households have been mobilized into Self-Help Groups (SHGs), fostering collective entrepreneurship.
• Special focus was given to women empowerment, with a majority of beneficiaries being women participating in micro-enterprises.
• The mission has played a crucial role in reducing urban poverty and informal sector vulnerabilities, contributing to inclusive urban growth.

articles.headline: Government Unveils National Cybersecurity Strategy 2025 for Digital Resilience
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) launched the comprehensive National Cybersecurity Strategy 2025.
• The strategy aims to enhance India's digital resilience against evolving cyber threats, protecting critical information infrastructure and citizen data.
• Key pillars include strengthening cybersecurity research & development, building a skilled workforce, promoting public-private partnerships, and fostering international cooperation.
• It mandates robust security audits for government agencies and critical sectors, along with a framework for rapid incident response.
• The strategy emphasizes a 'whole-of-nation' approach to secure India's digital ecosystem amidst increasing cyber-attacks and geopolitical tensions.

articles.headline: India's First Gigafactory for Lithium-Ion Cell Manufacturing Commences Production
articles.explainer:
• India's first indigenous gigafactory for Lithium-Ion (Li-ion) cell manufacturing officially commenced production in Karnataka.
• The facility, a major boost to the 'Make in India' initiative, aims to reduce India's reliance on imported Li-ion cells for electric vehicles (EVs) and energy storage.
• The factory utilizes advanced technology to produce high-density cells, catering to the rapidly growing EV market and renewable energy sector.
• This development is crucial for establishing a robust domestic supply chain for battery manufacturing and achieving energy security.
• It is expected to create thousands of jobs and position India as a key player in the global battery manufacturing landscape.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the recent Supreme Court judgment on 'Right to Digital Identity', consider the following statements:
1. The judgment declares 'Right to Digital Identity' as an intrinsic part of Article 19 of the Indian Constitution.
2. It mandates the government to ensure robust data protection frameworks for all digital platforms.
3. The ruling primarily applies to government agencies and does not extend to private entities handling citizen data.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Supreme Court declared the 'Right to Digital Identity' as an intrinsic part of the fundamental Right to Privacy under Article 21, not Article 19. The ruling emphasizes robust data protection frameworks for all digital platforms and services, implying it applies to both government agencies and private entities handling citizen data. Therefore, only statement 2 is correct.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following bodies is responsible for compiling and releasing the Index of Industrial Production (IIP) in India?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Index of Industrial Production (IIP) is compiled and released by the National Statistical Office (NSO) under the Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation (MoSPI).

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the Coastal Regulation Zone (CRZ) Notification:
1. CRZ rules are implemented by the Ministry of Ports, Shipping and Waterways.
2. The CRZ Notification classifies coastal areas into four categories based on ecological sensitivity and developmental activities.
3. The 'Blue Economy' concept is inherently incompatible with CRZ regulations.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: CRZ rules are implemented by the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change, not the Ministry of Ports, Shipping and Waterways (Statement 1 is incorrect). The CRZ Notification does classify coastal areas into four categories (CRZ-I, CRZ-II, CRZ-III, CRZ-IV) (Statement 2 is correct). The 'Blue Economy' concept aims for sustainable use of ocean resources, and recent amendments to CRZ seek to balance environmental protection with its promotion, implying compatibility, not incompatibility (Statement 3 is incorrect).

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following is NOT a component of India's National Quantum Mission (NQM)?
prelimsMocks.explanation: India's National Quantum Mission (NQM) focuses on scientific and technological development in quantum domains like Quantum Computing, Quantum Communication, Quantum Sensing and Metrology, and Quantum Materials. Quantum Astrology is not a scientific or technological domain and is not part of the mission.

prelimsMocks.question: With respect to India's Deep Ocean Mission, 'Matsya 6000' refers to:

(b) An underwater research laboratory in the Indian Ocean.
(c) India's indigenous manned submersible for deep-sea exploration.
(d) A new remote sensing satellite for oceanographic studies.

prelimsMocks.explanation: 'Matsya 6000' is the name of India's indigenous manned submersible being developed under the Samudrayaan project, part of the Deep Ocean Mission, designed to carry three personnel to a depth of 6,000 meters for deep-sea exploration.

GS1.headline: New Initiatives Launched to Preserve Endangered Tribal Languages in India
GS1.explainer:
• The Ministry of Tribal Affairs, in collaboration with linguistic experts, launched a series of initiatives to document and preserve India's endangered tribal languages.
• The program includes digital archiving of oral traditions, development of teaching materials, and training of community linguists.
• It aims to revitalize linguistic diversity, which is intrinsically linked to the cultural heritage and identity of tribal communities.
• The initiative will focus on languages with fewer than 10,000 speakers, leveraging technology to create accessible resources.
• This effort aligns with India's commitment to protecting indigenous cultures and promoting multilingualism.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Upholds 'Right to Digital Identity' as Fundamental Right
GS2.explainer:
• In a landmark judgment, the Supreme Court of India declared the 'Right to Digital Identity' as an intrinsic part of the fundamental Right to Privacy under Article 21.
• The ruling mandates the government to ensure robust data protection frameworks for all digital platforms and services.
• It emphasizes that individuals have control over their digital footprint, including the right to access, correct, and erase their personal data.
• The judgment sets a precedent for future legislation on data governance, digital public infrastructure, and the ethical use of AI.
• It has significant implications for both government agencies handling citizen data and private entities operating in the digital economy.

GS2.headline: Tripura Assembly Speaker Biswa Bandhu Sen Passes Away at 72
GS2.explainer:
• Biswa Bandhu Sen, the sitting Speaker of the Tripura Legislative Assembly, passed away at the age of 72 due to a brain hemorrhage.
• Sen had a long and distinguished career in state politics, serving as an MLA since 2008.
• He held the position of Deputy Speaker from 2018 to 2023 before being elected Speaker in 2023.
• His demise marks a significant loss for the political landscape of Tripura.
• Condolences poured in from across the political spectrum, highlighting his contributions to public service.

GS2.headline: India-ASEAN Dialogue Emphasizes Enhanced Maritime Security Cooperation
GS2.explainer:
• India and the Association of Southeast Asian Nations (ASEAN) concluded their annual strategic dialogue with a strong focus on bolstering maritime security.
• Discussions centered on joint patrols, information sharing, and capacity building to counter piracy, illegal fishing, and enhance disaster response in the Indo-Pacific.
• Both sides reiterated their commitment to a free, open, and rules-based Indo-Pacific region, adhering to international law, particularly UNCLOS.
• The dialogue also explored avenues for greater economic integration and connectivity projects, including the India-Myanmar-Thailand Trilateral Highway extension.
• This enhanced cooperation is seen as crucial for regional stability and economic prosperity amidst evolving geopolitical dynamics.

GS2.headline: Parliament Passes 'Prison Reforms Bill, 2025' to Modernize Correctional Facilities
GS2.explainer:
• Both Houses of Parliament passed the landmark 'Prison Reforms Bill, 2025', replacing the colonial-era Prisons Act of 1894.
• The new legislation focuses on the reformation and rehabilitation of prisoners, emphasizing vocational training, education, and psychological support.
• It mandates improved living conditions, healthcare access, and legal aid for inmates, aligning with international human rights standards.
• The Bill also introduces provisions for the use of technology in prison management, such as e-prisons and video conferencing for trials.
• States will now have a framework to adopt modern prison administration practices, addressing issues of overcrowding and staff training.

GS2.headline: India Reaffirms Commitment to UN Peacekeeping with Enhanced Contributions
GS2.explainer:
• India, a leading contributor to UN Peacekeeping missions, reaffirmed its commitment to global peace and security with additional pledges of personnel and equipment.
• During a high-level UN meeting, India emphasized the need for comprehensive reforms in UN Peacekeeping operations, including greater representation for troop-contributing countries.
• Discussions focused on addressing the evolving nature of conflicts, enhancing peacekeepers' safety, and integrating technology into missions.
• India highlighted its long-standing tradition of supporting UN efforts, having contributed over 260,000 troops to 49 missions since 1948.
• The pledges include specialized units for cybersecurity and medical support, reflecting modern peacekeeping demands.

GS2.imageDescription: Indian peacekeepers in blue helmets standing in formation under the UN flag.
GS2.trivia: India is one of the few countries to have deployed all-women contingents in UN Peacekeeping missions, setting a precedent for gender equality.

GS2.headline: National Health Mission 2025: Progress Report Highlights Achievements and Gaps
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare released its annual progress report for the National Health Mission (NHM) 2025, detailing achievements and identifying areas for improvement.
• The report highlighted significant strides in maternal and child health indicators, including reduced Infant Mortality Rate (IMR) and Maternal Mortality Ratio (MMR).
• Focus areas for improvement include strengthening primary healthcare infrastructure, increasing human resources in rural areas, and addressing non-communicable diseases (NCDs).
• Digital health initiatives, such as the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM), showed accelerated adoption, enhancing healthcare access and efficiency.
• The report also underscored the need for greater public-private partnerships to achieve universal health coverage goals.

GS2.headline: Lokpal Strengthens Anti-Corruption Framework with New Guidelines for Public Officials
GS2.explainer:
• The Lokpal of India issued a comprehensive set of new guidelines aimed at strengthening the anti-corruption framework for public officials.
• The guidelines mandate stricter compliance with asset declaration rules, enhanced vigilance mechanisms, and quicker disposal of corruption complaints.
• They also emphasize a proactive approach to prevent corruption through systemic reforms and technology integration.
• The move is expected to improve transparency and accountability in public administration, fostering a culture of integrity.
• The Lokpal reiterated its commitment to acting against corruption at the highest levels of government, ensuring ethical governance.

GS2.headline: India-EU Free Trade Agreement Negotiations Show Positive Momentum
GS2.explainer:
• Negotiations for the India-European Union Free Trade Agreement (FTA) have shown significant positive momentum in the latest round of talks.
• Both sides reported progress on key areas including market access for goods, services, and investment protection.
• The FTA aims to boost bilateral trade and investment, creating new opportunities for businesses and fostering economic growth.
• Remaining contentious issues primarily revolve around tariffs on sensitive agricultural products and intellectual property rights.
• A joint statement expressed optimism for a comprehensive and mutually beneficial agreement by late 2026, marking a major milestone in India-EU relations.

GS2.headline: India and Iran Review Progress on Chabahar Port Expansion, Regional Connectivity
GS2.explainer:
• India and Iran held a high-level review meeting to assess the progress of the Chabahar Port development project and its role in regional connectivity.
• Both nations reiterated their commitment to expediting the operationalization of the Shahid Beheshti terminal, managed by India.
• Discussions focused on streamlining logistical challenges, attracting further investment, and expanding the port's role as a transit hub for Afghanistan and Central Asian countries.
• The port is crucial for India to bypass Pakistan for trade with Afghanistan and Central Asia, enhancing its strategic influence.
• The meeting also explored avenues for broader bilateral economic cooperation, including energy and infrastructure projects.

GS2.headline: NULM Report Highlights Positive Impact on Urban Poor Livelihoods
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs released a comprehensive report on the impact of the National Urban Livelihoods Mission (NULM).
• The report highlighted significant achievements in skill development, self-employment, and access to financial services for the urban poor.
• Over 10 million urban poor households have been mobilized into Self-Help Groups (SHGs), fostering collective entrepreneurship.
• Special focus was given to women empowerment, with a majority of beneficiaries being women participating in micro-enterprises.
• The mission has played a crucial role in reducing urban poverty and informal sector vulnerabilities, contributing to inclusive urban growth.

GS3.headline: RBI MPC Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate Amidst Easing Inflation Concerns
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) unanimously decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at 6.5% in its December review.
• The decision comes amidst a gradual easing of retail inflation, which has moved closer to the RBI's target band, and moderate economic growth.
• RBI Governor highlighted global uncertainties and the need to remain vigilant against potential inflationary pressures from food prices and crude oil.
• The MPC reiterated its commitment to aligning inflation with the 4% target while supporting growth.
• Analysts anticipate a possible rate cut in early 2026 if inflation continues its downward trajectory and global economic conditions stabilize.

GS3.imageDescription: Reserve Bank of India building facade, symbolizing monetary policy decisions.
GS3.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee was established in 2016 to bring more transparency and accountability to monetary policy decisions.

GS3.headline: Government Unveils National Circular Economy Policy to Boost Sustainability
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change launched the comprehensive National Circular Economy Policy (NCEP) 2025.
• The policy aims to minimize waste, maximize resource utilization, and promote sustainable production and consumption patterns across various sectors.
• Key pillars include extended producer responsibility (EPR), material efficiency, waste-to-wealth initiatives, and green procurement.
• It sets specific targets for resource recovery rates in sectors like plastics, electronics, construction & demolition, and automotive.
• The NCEP is expected to drive green innovation, create new jobs, and reduce India's reliance on virgin resources, contributing to climate goals.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches Advanced Communication Satellite 'GSAT-N3'
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully launched its next-generation communication satellite, GSAT-N3, from Sriharikota.
• Carried aboard the GSLV Mk-III (LVM3) rocket, GSAT-N3 is designed to provide high-throughput communication services for over 15 years.
• The satellite will enhance broadband connectivity, disaster management support, and satellite-based navigation services across India and neighboring regions.
• This mission marks a significant step in India's efforts to augment its indigenous satellite communication capabilities.
• The advanced features include higher capacity transponders and improved spectral efficiency, crucial for meeting growing data demands.

GS3.trivia: GSLV Mk-III, now designated LVM3 (Launch Vehicle Mark-3), is ISRO's heaviest launch vehicle, capable of launching 4-tonne class satellites into GTO.

GS3.headline: India's Industrial Production Records Moderate Growth in Q3 FY26
GS3.explainer:
• India's Index of Industrial Production (IIP) registered a moderate growth of 4.2% in the third quarter (October-December) of FY26.
• The manufacturing sector remained the primary driver, albeit with mixed performance across sub-sectors.
• Mining output showed robust expansion, while electricity generation maintained a steady pace.
• The data indicates a cautious recovery trend, influenced by global demand fluctuations and domestic consumption patterns.
• Government initiatives like Make in India and PLI schemes are expected to further boost industrial activity in the coming quarters.

GS3.headline: India Announces Ambitious New Targets for Renewable Energy Capacity by 2030
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy announced enhanced targets for India's renewable energy (RE) capacity, aiming for 550 GW by 2030.
• This revised target includes significant increases in solar, wind, and hydropower capacities, demonstrating India's commitment to climate action.
• The announcement emphasizes accelerating green hydrogen production and energy storage solutions to support grid stability.
• Policy measures will focus on incentivizing private investment, streamlining regulatory approvals, and promoting domestic manufacturing of RE components.
• The move aligns with India's nationally determined contributions (NDCs) under the Paris Agreement and aims to reduce reliance on fossil fuels.

GS3.headline: Accelerated Border Management Infrastructure Development in Northeast
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Home Affairs announced accelerated plans for border management infrastructure development along India's northeastern frontiers.
• The initiative includes constructing new border outposts, upgrading existing surveillance technology, and improving road connectivity to remote areas.
• The focus is on enhancing security, facilitating legitimate trade, and addressing cross-border challenges like illegal immigration and smuggling.
• This development is part of a broader strategy to strengthen India's presence and foster economic growth in the border regions.
• Local communities are being engaged to ensure inclusive development and address their specific needs while improving security.

GS3.headline: SEBI Introduces Stricter Disclosure Norms for ESG Funds
GS3.explainer:
• The Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) announced tighter disclosure and compliance norms for Environment, Social, and Governance (ESG) funds.
• The new regulations aim to prevent 'greenwashing' and ensure greater transparency and accountability in ESG investing.
• Asset Management Companies (AMCs) managing ESG funds will be required to disclose detailed methodologies, criteria for stock selection, and impact assessment reports.
• This move is expected to boost investor confidence in ESG products and channel more capital towards genuinely sustainable investments.
• The framework also includes provisions for independent third-party verification of ESG claims and standardized reporting formats.

GS3.headline: National Quantum Mission Achieves Breakthrough in Quantum Computing Hardware
GS3.explainer:
• Scientists under India's National Quantum Mission (NQM) announced a significant breakthrough in developing stable quantum computing hardware.
• Researchers at a premier institute successfully demonstrated a 32-qubit superconducting quantum processor with enhanced coherence times.
• This achievement positions India among the few nations capable of developing advanced quantum computing technologies.
• The breakthrough is crucial for advancing quantum computing applications in drug discovery, materials science, and cryptography.
• NQM aims to make India a leader in quantum technologies, fostering indigenous research and development across various quantum domains.

GS3.headline: Coastal Regulation Zone Amendments to Boost 'Blue Economy' and Coastal Development
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change announced significant amendments to the Coastal Regulation Zone (CRZ) Notification.
• The amendments aim to balance environmental protection with the promotion of 'Blue Economy' initiatives and sustainable coastal development.
• Key changes include simplified procedures for coastal infrastructure projects, promotion of eco-tourism, and clearer guidelines for local communities' livelihoods.
• The revised CRZ framework seeks to facilitate investment in port development, renewable energy projects (offshore wind), and aquaculture.
• Environmental safeguards, including stringent EIA norms and protection of ecologically sensitive areas, remain central to the amended policy.

GS3.headline: India's Digital Payments Ecosystem Records Record Transactions in Q3 FY26
GS3.explainer:

• UPI (Unified Payments Interface) continued to be the dominant force, accounting for a major share of the transactions, driven by merchant payments and peer-to-peer transfers.

GS3.headline: India's Deep Ocean Mission: Samudrayaan Project Nears Crucial Submersible Trials
GS3.explainer:
• India's ambitious Deep Ocean Mission, particularly the Samudrayaan project, is nearing crucial manned submersible trials in early 2026.
• The 'Matsya 6000' submersible, designed to carry three personnel to a depth of 6,000 meters, has completed integration and shallow-water tests.
• This mission aims to explore deep-sea resources like polymetallic nodules and conduct biodiversity studies in the central Indian Ocean basin.
• It marks a significant step towards India's capability in deep-sea exploration and sustainable utilization of ocean resources.
• The project also involves the development of advanced ocean technologies and a comprehensive understanding of marine ecosystems.

GS3.trivia: India has been allocated a site of 75,000 sq km in the Central Indian Ocean Basin by the International Seabed Authority for the exploration of polymetallic nodules.

GS3.headline: Namami Gange Mission Enters Phase III with Focus on Sustainable River Basin Management
GS3.explainer:
• The Namami Gange Mission has officially entered its third phase, shifting focus from initial cleanup efforts to long-term sustainable river basin management.
• Phase III emphasizes the integration of ecological restoration, biodiversity conservation, and community participation in maintaining the Ganga's health.
• New projects include advanced wastewater treatment plants, wetland rejuvenation, afforestation along the river banks, and promotion of eco-friendly agricultural practices.
• The mission aims to establish a robust governance framework for inter-state and inter-sectoral coordination for holistic river management.
• This phase also prioritizes scientific research and data-driven approaches to monitor river health and address emerging challenges like climate change impacts.

GS3.headline: Government Unveils National Cybersecurity Strategy 2025 for Digital Resilience
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) launched the comprehensive National Cybersecurity Strategy 2025.
• The strategy aims to enhance India's digital resilience against evolving cyber threats, protecting critical information infrastructure and citizen data.
• Key pillars include strengthening cybersecurity research & development, building a skilled workforce, promoting public-private partnerships, and fostering international cooperation.
• It mandates robust security audits for government agencies and critical sectors, along with a framework for rapid incident response.
• The strategy emphasizes a 'whole-of-nation' approach to secure India's digital ecosystem amidst increasing cyber-attacks and geopolitical tensions.

GS3.headline: India's First Gigafactory for Lithium-Ion Cell Manufacturing Commences Production
GS3.explainer:
• India's first indigenous gigafactory for Lithium-Ion (Li-ion) cell manufacturing officially commenced production in Karnataka.
• The facility, a major boost to the 'Make in India' initiative, aims to reduce India's reliance on imported Li-ion cells for electric vehicles (EVs) and energy storage.
• The factory utilizes advanced technology to produce high-density cells, catering to the rapidly growing EV market and renewable energy sector.
• This development is crucial for establishing a robust domestic supply chain for battery manufacturing and achieving energy security.
• It is expected to create thousands of jobs and position India as a key player in the global battery manufacturing landscape.
